writes.content: Rust's ownership model traces its roots back to the RAII idiom from C++
C++ smart pointers like shared_ptr are built on reference counting
Rust's Rc type is similar to shared_ptr but is not thread-safe
Arc is Rust's atomic reference-counted smart pointer
Reference counting is commonly used when modeling graph data structures
Rust's borrow checker eliminates the need for reference counting in many cases
Swift also uses automatic reference counting, abbreviated as ARC
Garbage collection is the main alternative to reference counting for memory management

query.text: Rust reference counting and ownership model
